In the [48;2;51;51;51m[37mgrades.csv[39m[49m file, the [48;2;51;51;51m[37mScience[39m[49m subject has only one score ([48;2;51;51;51m[37m88.0[39m[49m), which causes this error.

To fix this, I will modify the [48;2;51;51;51m[37msummarize[39m[49m function to handle cases where there is only one data point. If there is only one score, the standard deviation will be set to [48;2;51;51;51m[37m0.0[39m[49m (or omitted, but setting it to [48;2;51;51;51m[37m0.0[39m[49m is a common approach for single data points).

args.old_text:
def summarize(grades):
    lines = []
    for subject in sorted(grades):
        scores = grades[subject]
        avg = statistics.mean(scores)
        std = statistics.stdev(scores)
        lines.append(f"{subject}: avg={avg:.1f} std={std:.2f}")
    return lines
args.new_text:
def summarize(grades):
    lines = []
    for subject in sorted(grades):
        scores = grades[subject]
        avg = statistics.mean(scores)
        std = statistics.stdev(scores) if len(scores) > 1 else 0.0
        lines.append(f"{subject}: avg={avg:.1f} std={std:.2f}")
    return lines
